title: ‘The Last Day’ Review: Alicia Vikander Leads This ‘Mrs. Dalloway’ Interpretation, but Victoria Pedretti’s Performance Steals the Show
description: Tribeca: Rachel Rose’s directorial debut puts a modern spin on Virginia Woolf’s classic novel, centering on two women who navigate motherhood in different — and sometimes eerily similar — ways.
hook: Virginia Woolf meets motherhood in a modern film where two women's days eerily mirror each other—and Victoria Pedretti steals every scene.

title: “Rafa”: the Destruction of a Man, and the Making of a Legend
description: For tennis champion Rafael Nadal, pain has always felt like weakness leaving the body, and a new Netflix docuseries shows the boons of this ideology, as well as its undeniable costs.
hook: Nadal's pain philosophy made him a legend, but Netflix's docuseries reveals what that ideology actually costs—glory built on suffering.

title: To Reach Beyond Ourselves Is Key to Our Survival
description:
“We wanted to make a book that began to answer the questions Muriel Rukeyser asked. We wanted to make a book that continued to ask the questions she asked, of ourselves, of each other.”

The post To Reach Beyond Ourselves Is Key to Our Survival appeared first on Public Books.
hook: How Muriel Rukeyser's radical questions about survival and connection shaped a new generation of thinkers asking the same urgent things.

title: The First 18 Months
description: A Cabinet meeting with my son, who is exactly as old as the current administration
hook: A mother's cabinet meetings with her toddler expose the absurd governance happening in the smallest domestic spaces.

title: Coastal Time: New Fiction by M. John Harrison
description:
In an exclusive extract from his new book The End of Everything, the Warwickshire-born SF visionary pictures the apocalypse, as seen from the shores of Kent

In the beginning – if there could be said to have been a beginning – no one really understood what had happened.

There was a decade of confusion. Populations declined across the board, as
hook: M. John Harrison pictures the apocalypse from a Kent shore—a dark, atmospheric glimpse of civilization's final hours.

title: Marjane Satrapi captured profound human emotions – and paved the way for a generation
description:
The graphic novelist had a remarkable gift for visual storytelling, in the phenomenon that was Persepolis and beyond. Many of us owe our careers to the space she created, says Iranian cartoonist Mana Neyestani

• News: Marjane Satrapi, creator of Persepolis and acclaimed French-Iranian artist, dies aged 56

On the morning of 4 June, when
hook: Marjane Satrapi didn't just make Persepolis—she cracked open a space for an entire generation of visual storytellers.

title: How City Kids Used to Play on the Streets of New York
description: A retrospective of Martha Cooper’s work shows the ramshackle toys and improvised games from a bygone era of growing up.
hook: Martha Cooper's photographs capture the feral ingenuity of '70s New York kids—before screens, before supervision, pure improvisation.
